ontology.analysis_summary: The BK21 Hanyang University graduate program is a large-scale Korean government initiative to enhance graduate research capacity and national competitiveness. It funds education research groups and graduate innovation universities, providing scholarships and salaries to graduate students and early-career researchers. The policy ecosystem involves the Ministry of Education as funder, the National Research Foundation as evaluator and manager, university administrations as implementers, and project oversight committees as decision bodies. Key processes include annual evaluations, mid-term evaluations, compliance checks, and consulting. Information flows through evaluation reports and eligibility criteria, while resources flow as research scholarships and innovation support funds. The ontology captures the core actors, positions, resources, criteria, and reports needed for simulation.
